sections.paragraph:
καταγράφω impf. κατέγραφον; fut. καταγράψω Hos 8:12; aor. κατέγραψα LXX. Pass.: aor. κατεγράφην LXX; pf. ptc. καταγεγραμμένοι LXX (s. γράφω; Eur., Pla. et al.; ins, pap, LXX) write, also draw figures (so Paus. 1, 28, 2; Serenus Antinoensis [IV a.d.] p. 280, 13; 282, 22 al. [ILHeiberg 1896].—But καταγρ. also means ‘write down an accusation’: PCairZen 140, 17 [256 b.c.] κατὰ τούτων καταγέγραφέν σοι) εἰς τὴν γῆν on the ground J 8:6 (v.l. ἔγραφεν), vs. 8 D. For ins s. KMiller, Apollo Lairbenos: Numen 32, ’85, 46–70; e.g. MAMA IV, 275 B I, 4 (II a.d.).—On the subject matter s. Diog. L. 2, 127: Menedemus the philosopher (300 b.c.), in whose presence someone behaved improperly, διέγραφεν εἰς τοὔδαφος (=drew a cartoon on the ground [and thereby shamed him]).—M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Paus Paus , perieg., II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

MAMA MAMA = Monumenta Asiae Minoris Antiqua—List 3

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 516
